ip_outcome_note: Confirmed via Scryfall: type_line is 'Legendary Planeswalker — Oko' and the card is named 'Oko, the Trickster'. The depicted figure is the planeswalker Oko himself; this is one of two Throne of Eldraine printings of the character (the other is the famously-banned 'Oko, Thief of Crowns', ELD-197). The art (antlered shirtless figure conjuring twin green/pink energy ribbons) matches Oko's canonical visual identifiers — antler-crown (his Shadowmoor aspect feature per MTG Wiki) and shapeshifter / fey-trickster framing. Oko is a Wizards-internal IP (Magic: The Gathering original character from Throne of Eldraine, 2019), not an external crossover risk, so no '## IP' callout block is required — only ip_verified flips to true.

trivia_bullets.text: This is the planeswalker-deck-exclusive printing of Oko (ELD-309), reprinted on The List (PLST) on 2023-09-08 to coincide with the Wilds of Eldraine launch. The List is Wizards' rotating reprint-in-boosters pool; cards from it are legal in whatever formats they were already legal in [Scryfall, via WebSearch snippet of magic.wizards.com 'What's New on The List for Wilds of Eldraine'].
sources: magic.wizards.com: What's New on The List for Wilds of Eldraine

trivia_bullets.text: Art by Chris Rallis, a Greek-born fantasy illustrator based in England who has worked almost exclusively for Wizards of the Coast since 2013 after Jeremy Jarvis picked up his portfolio; ~158 MTG cards to his name, including Liliana, Death's Majesty and Dragon Whisperer [Chris Rallis: rallisart.com about page; Dragon Shield artist interview, via WebSearch snippets].

Not the banned Oko — important distinction. 'Oko, the Trickster' is the weaker planeswalker-deck variant: six mana ({4}{G}{U}), 4 loyalty, abilities are +1/+1 counters, copy-a-creature, and a -7 ultimate that makes your team 10/10 trample. 'Oko, Thief of Crowns' (ELD-197, the famously-banned one) costs three mana ({1}{G}{U}) and does roughly the same job a turn earlier with Food tokens and Elk-conversion; that card was banned in Standard (2019-11-18), Brawl, Pioneer, and Modern within months of release for warping every format it touched [Scryfall card pages for both prints; magic.wizards.com Banned and Restricted Announcement, November 18, 2019].
sources: Scryfall: oracle text + legalities for both Oko prints

trivia_bullets.text: EDHREC rank 16704 — essentially unplayed in Commander, which tracks: planeswalker-deck exclusives are typically casual-precon stat sticks, not staples. The List reprint trades around USD 0.90 [Scryfall].

The depicted figure is Oko, a shapeshifter fey planeswalker originally from Lorwyn-Shadowmoor; his Shadowmoor aspect grows antlers atop his head, which is the visual cue this art leans on. He is charismatic, vain, and a notorious trickster; in the Throne of Eldraine story arc he met the human hedgewitch Alyse and fathered the half-fae Kellan [MTG Wiki: Oko, via WebSearch snippet].
sources: mtg.fandom.com/wiki/Oko (via WebSearch snippet — direct fetch blocked by Cloudflare)

related_cards.name: Oko, Thief of Crowns
related_cards.note: The other Oko print from the same set — the famously-banned three-mana mythic. Same character, vastly stronger card.

related_cards.name: Oko's Hospitality
related_cards.note: Companion piece in the same planeswalker deck — listed as a related card by Scryfall ('combo_piece'); part of the Oko, the Trickster planeswalker deck product.

related_cards.name: Oko, the Ringleader
related_cards.note: Oko's return printing on his home plane during the Wilds of Eldraine set window when this List reprint was inserted. Same character, different card.

related_cards.name: Kellan, the Fae-Blooded
related_cards.note: Oko's half-fae son per MTG Wiki lore — directly tied to Oko's Eldraine story arc.
